prompt: Write two sentences describing how it feels when someone asks you the same thing six different ways hoping for a different answer.
response: When someone asks me the same thing six different ways hoping for a different answer, it can be both frustrating and demoralizing. Frustrating because it often feels like my initial response was not understood or valued, and demoralizing because it may make me question the clarity of my communication or my ability to provide a satisfactory answer.
